parameters.query: SELECT id, message, source, level, created_at FROM sn_error_logs WHERE level IN ('error','critical') AND created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '15 minutes' AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM sn_error_encyclopedia WHERE LOWER(error_pattern) LIKE '%' || LOWER(SUBSTRING(sn_error_logs.message,1,50)) || '%') ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 5

parameters.name: messages
parameters.value: =[{"role":"system","content":"You are a ServiceNow error expert. Analyze errors and return ONLY JSON: {\"title\":\"short name\",\"description\":\"what it means\",\"root_cause\":\"why it happens\",\"fix_steps\":[\"step1\",\"step2\"],\"category\":\"Script|API|Flow|DB|Auth|Spoke|Platform\",\"severity\":\"low|medium|high|critical\"}"},{"role":"user","content":"ServiceNow error from {{ $json.source }}: {{ $json.message }}"}]

parameters.query: INSERT INTO sn_error_encyclopedia (error_pattern, title, description, root_cause, fix_steps, category, severity, source) VALUES (SUBSTRING($1, 1, 200), $2, $3, $4, $5::jsonb, $6, $7, 'ai_generated') ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING RETURNING id

parameters.jsCode: return [{ json: { success: true, message: 'No new errors to process' } }];
